Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–499, title V, § 517(e)100 Stat. 1774Oct. 17, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section, amending , The Public Health and Welfare, and repealing sections 9631 to 9633 of Title 42] shall take effect on .section 9601 of Title 42January 1, 1987“(2).—Superfund treated as continuation of old trust fundThe Hazardous Substance Superfund established by the amendments made by this section shall be treated for all purposes of law as a continuation of the Hazardous Substance Response Trust Fund established by section 221 of the Hazardous Substance Response Revenue Act of 1980 [former ]. Any reference in any law to the Hazardous Substance Response Trust Fund established by such section 221 shall be deemed to include (wherever appropriate) a reference to the Hazardous Substance Superfund established by the amendments made by this section.”42 U.S.C. 9631

G, title IV, § 443(b)136 Stat. 4833Dec. 29, 2022“(b)For this fiscal year [Fiscal Year 2023] and each fiscal year thereafter, such sums as are available in the Hazardous Substance Superfund established under section 9507 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 at the end of the preceding fiscal year from taxes received in the Treasury under subsection (b)(1) of such section shall be available, without further appropriation, to remain available until expended, to be used to carry out the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 ( et seq.): , That the amount provided by this subsection is designated by the Congress as being for an emergency requirement pursuant to section 4001(a)(1) of S. children.text: appropriated to the Superfund as provided in this section,
appropriated to the Superfund pursuant to section 517(b) of the Superfund Revenue Act of 1986, or
credited to the Superfund as provided in section 9602(b).
the taxes received in the Treasury under section 4611, 4661, or 4671 (relating to environmental taxes),
amounts recovered on behalf of the Superfund under the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 (hereinafter in this section referred to as “CERCLA”),
all moneys recovered or collected under section 311(b)(6)(B) of the Clean Water Act,1See References in Text note below.1
penalties assessed under title I of CERCLA, and
punitive damages under section 107(c)(3) of CERCLA.

children.label: i
children.text: paragraphs (1), (2), (5), and (6) of section 111(a) of CERCLA as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Superfund Amendments and Reauthorization Act of 1986,

children.label: ii
children.text: section 111(c) of CERCLA (as so in effect), other than paragraphs (1) and (2) thereof, and

children.label: iii
children.text: section 111(m) of CERCLA (as so in effect), or

children.label: B
children.text: hereafter authorized by a law which does not authorize the expenditure out of the Superfund for a general purpose not covered by subparagraph (A) (as so in effect).

children.heading: Exception for certain transfers, etc., of hazardous substances
children.label: 2 Exception for certain transfers, etc., of hazardous substances
A
children.text: the transfer or disposal, if made on , would not comply with a State or local requirement,December 13, 1985

children.label: B
children.text: the transfer is to a facility for which a final permit under section 3005(a) of the Solid Waste Disposal Act was issued after , and before , andJanuary 1, 1983November 1, 1984

children.label: C
children.text: the transfer is from a facility identified as the McColl Site in Fullerton, California.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Superfund, as repayable advances, such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of the Superfund.

children.heading: Limitation on aggregate advances
children.label: 2 Limitation on aggregate advances
children.text: The maximum aggregate amount of repayable advances to the Superfund which is outstanding at any one time shall not exceed an amount equal to the amount which the Secretary estimates will be equal to the sum of the amounts appropriated to the Superfund under subsection (b)(1) during the following 24 months.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Advances made to the Superfund shall be repaid, and interest on such advances shall be paid, to the general fund of the Treasury when the Secretary determines that moneys are available for such purposes in the Superfund.

children.heading: Final repayment
children.label: B Final repayment
children.text: No advance shall be made to the Superfund after , and all advances to such Fund shall be repaid on or before such date.December 31, 2032

children.heading: Rate of interest
children.label: C Rate of interest
children.text: Interest on advances made to the Superfund shall be at a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury (as of the close of the calendar month preceding the month in which the advance is made) to be equal to the current average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States with remaining periods to maturity comparable to the anticipated period during which the advance will be outstanding and shall be compounded annually.

children.heading: Liability of United States limited to amount in Trust Fund
children.label: e Liability of United States limited to amount in Trust Fund

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Any claim filed against the Superfund may be paid only out of the Superfund.

children.heading: Coordination with other provisions
children.label: 2 Coordination with other provisions
children.text: Nothing in CERCLA or the Superfund Amendments and Reauthorization Act of 1986 (or in any amendment made by either of such Acts) shall authorize the payment by the United States Government of any amount with respect to any such claim out of any source other than the Superfund.

children.heading: Order in which unpaid claims are to be paid
children.label: 3 Order in which unpaid claims are to be paid
children.text: If at any time the Superfund has insufficient funds to pay all of the claims payable out of the Superfund at such time, such claims shall, to the extent permitted under paragraph (1), be paid in full in the order in which they were finally determined.
